text:
There once was a woman who had lost everything she loved. Money. Health. Friends. Her life was gone.

She wandered the streets, searching every place for something of value. That's when she found them—the coins on the sidewalk.

Each one felt like a whisper to her lost heart. So, she picked them up, gathering treasures. And then, she visited a little antique shop.

"Any coin collectors here?" she asked the shopkeeper.

He grinned. "Oh yeah. But I've never held something like that!"

She handed over a pile. "They're sea hares."

He chuckled. "You'd make a lovely collector!"

That spring, they visited grave sites, exchanging coins.

One day, she discovered a woman sitting beside an old grave.

"You've got some interesting pieces," she remarked.

The woman smiled cryptically. "Do you never give up?"

"No. Why?" she asked, puzzled.

"That place isn't just a grave."

Her ears perked.
